content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was built

### 1. `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` *(new)*
Defines the `Theme` interface and exports `listThemes()` / `getTheme(id)`:

- **`ThemeColors`** — 7 named color slots: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar`
- **`Theme`** — adds `id`, `name`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`, and `colorScheme: "dark" | "light"` (needed to drive the `.dark` CSS class)
- **Three built-in themes:**
  - **`t3-dark`** (default) — near-black background, pure oklch neutrals, indigo accent
  - **`t3-light`** — white background, charcoal text, same indigo accent
  - **`t3-midnight`** — deep navy-tinged dark, slightly warmer blue accent

### 2. `packages/shared/package.json` *(updated)*
Added `"./themes"` subpath export so the web app can import with `@t3tools/shared/themes`.

### 3. `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` *(replaced)*
- **Module-level store** (same `useSyncExternalStore` pattern as before) applies the correct theme at module-load time — no FOUC.
- **`applyThemeToDom()`** toggles `.dark`/`.light` on `<html>`, then writes all 7 color tokens plus `--primary` / `--ring` / `--sidebar-background` / `--editor-background` / `--terminal-background` / `--status-bar-background` as CSS custom properties directly on `document.documentElement`.
- **`ThemeProvider`** — React context provider; exported and wired into the router's `Wrap` hierarchy.
- **`useTheme()`** returns `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes, resolvedTheme }`. The `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` field is preserved so all existing diff/syntax-highlight components continue to work unchanged.

### 4. `apps/web/src/router.ts` *(updated)*
`ThemeProvider` added to the `Wrap` chain: `QueryClientProvider → StoreProvider → ThemeProvider → children`.

### 5. `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
A self-contained `ThemeSelector` component that:
- Renders each theme as a `role="radio"` card
- Shows a row of 7 color swatches (one per `ThemeColors` key) as small circles
- Marks the active theme with an "Active" badge
- Calls `setTheme(id)` on click

### 6. `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(updated)*
The Appearance section's hand-rolled light/dark/system radio buttons are replaced with a single `<ThemeSelector />` call. The `resolvedTheme` label below it now reads "Color scheme: dark/light" to reflect the new model.
